 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-3z2","title":"fcp.spotify: Library Management","description":"# fcp.spotify: Library Management (FCP2)

## Goal
Enable safe access to the user’s library:
- list saved tracks/albums/shows
- save/unsave items

Library writes modify user state and must be auditable.

## Scope
- Get saved tracks/albums/shows/episodes (pagination).
- Save/unsave items.
- Check whether items are saved.

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `spotify.library.read`
- `spotify.library.write` (dangerous-ish)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- For writes:
  - receipts + audit events include item ids and action
  - never log track names or personal context

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `spotify.library.tracks.list`
  - `spotify.library.tracks.save`
  - `spotify.library.tracks.remove`
  - `spotify.library.albums.list`
  - `spotify.library.albums.save/remove`
  - `spotify.library.shows.list/save/remove`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - pagination
  - save/unsave idempotency
  - audit/receipt for writes

## Acceptance criteria
- Library operations are reliable, safe, and auditable.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-3zhv","title":"[IDEA-01.4] SDK runtime kit: tests + reference examples","description":"## Goal
Provide reference examples and comprehensive tests for the runtime kit.

## Details
- Add a minimal streaming example (fake gateway) and polling example (fake API) in testkit.
- Add golden-log snapshots for health transitions (dependent on flywheel_connectors-11g2 schema).
- Document usage patterns in `docs/STUDY_Clawdbot_Connector_Patterns.md` and/or SDK README.

## Tests
- Unit + integration tests for both supervisors.
- Validate logging against JSONL schema (once available).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Examples compile and can be used as copy/paste templates by connector authors.
- Tests cover common failure modes (timeouts, 429, disconnect, resume).","notes":"[AlienArtifactPractical]
- Add evidence ledger fields with per-check log-odds contributions and explicit loss decision.
- Add anytime-valid risk control (e-process) for approvals/denials and record epsilon in receipts.
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Compile policies to DAG/BDD and cache allow/deny results with LRU.
- Use bitset capability sets for fast subset checks.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-423bu.3.3","title":"[E2E/invoke] Verify capability token verification happens in the invoke chain","description":"Prove that fcp-host actually verifies capability tokens before dispatching. TOKEN MINTING FOR TESTS: Use the fcp-crypto COSE/CWT API to programmatically create test tokens. Generate a test Ed25519 keypair. Set FCP_HOST_CAPABILITY_PUBLIC_KEY=<hex-encoded-public-key> when starting fcp-host (code: resolve_verifying_key at fcp-host.rs:2002). Sign tokens with the private key. TEST CASES: 1) Valid token (correct zone, not expired, valid signature) -> invoke succeeds, 2) Expired token (exp in the past) -> rejected with error, 3) Not-yet-valid token (nbf in the future) -> rejected, 4) Wrong zone binding (token says z:public, connector bound to z:work) -> rejected, 5) Invalid signature (sign with DIFFERENT key than the one fcp-host trusts) -> rejected with SIGNATURE_INVALID, 6) Token missing the required operation capability -> rejected with CAPABILITY_DENIED. When capability_verifying_key is NOT set, fcp-host skips token verification entirely (code: 'let Some(host_public_key) = self.capability_verifying_key else { return Ok(()) }' at line 198). So this test MUST set the env var to prove enforcement is real, not just that the happy path works without keys.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:48:48.788984Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-07T01:28:08.312148Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T01:28:07.781499Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-423bu.3.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-423bu.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:48:48.788984Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-423bu.3.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-423bu.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:48:49.626413Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2509,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-423bu.3.3","author":"jemanuel","text":"IMPORTANT DISTINCTION: capability_token is ALWAYS required in the InvokeRequest JSON (deserialization fails without it).  {"id":"flywheel_connectors-thy","title":"fcp.twilio: SMS sending and receiving","description":"# fcp.twilio: SMS sending and receiving (FCP2)

## Goal
Provide core SMS/MMS capabilities:
- send outbound SMS (and MMS when enabled)
- read message status/history
- support inbound SMS via webhook-driven event flow (ingest handled in `flywheel_connectors-ugp`)

## Scope
### Outbound
- Send SMS to a single destination.
- Optional: bulk send with batching + rate limiting.
- Support idempotency keys to prevent duplicate sends.

### Status / history
- Get message details by SID.
- List messages with filters (date range, to/from).

### Media (MMS)
- List/download media attachments (bounded + host pinned).

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `twilio.sms.send`
- `twilio.sms.read`
- `twilio.sms.media.read`

Policy guidance:
- Outbound send is a real-world side effect; many orgs require approval for certain destinations.

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned to Twilio API host(s) and explicit media hosts.
- For sends:
  - receipts + audit events with message SID + to/from hashes (never raw phone numbers in logs)
  - strict log redaction for phone numbers and message bodies
- Taint model:
  - inbound message bodies are tainted by default

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `twilio.sms.send`
  - `twilio.sms.get`
  - `twilio.sms.list`
  - `twilio.sms.media.list`
  - `twilio.sms.media.download`
- Idempotency strategy:
  - stable client idempotency key → dedupe cache/state

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - send request formation
  - idempotency behavior
  - status polling
  - media download size limits
  - secret/PII redaction in logs

## Acceptance criteria
- Duplicate sends are mechanically prevented via idempotency.
- Logs and receipts never leak phone numbers or message bodies.
